mapping.a_element: eigensystem (eigenvalues) of the Jacobian used to build the kernel set
mapping.b_element: spectrum (eigenvalues) of the underlying graph / Grover walk operator

mapping.a_element: non-online reduced representation derived from the spectral decomposition (tabulated kernel set)
mapping.b_element: effective Hermitian matrix / continuous-time generator derived from the spectral structure

brief_justification: The only real overlap is the generic use of a matrix eigen/spectral decomposition; the transferable machinery differs entirely. Paper A's method is hash-table memoization (retrieval from a precomputed lookup table replacing online eigen-decomposition) to accelerate a stiff-ODE integrator, whereas Paper B's method is degenerate perturbation theory that yields an effective Hermitian generator characterizing the robustness of a periodic quantum walk — no shared governing process or role-for-role mapping beyond generic linear algebra.
